---
title: "Sahil Imtiyaz"
page-layout: full
---

# Sahil Imtiyaz

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-7}

**Postdoctoral Researcher / Postdoc Fellow**  
[Basque Center for Applied Mathematics (BCAM)](https://www.bcamath.org/es/node/11987)  
Mathematical, Computational and Experimental Neuroscience Group  

**Junior Scientist**  
[Canadian Quantum Research Center](https://candqrc.ca/people.php)  

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-5}

::: {.card}
::: {.card-body}

### Quick Links

[CV](cv.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}  
[Contact](contact.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}  
[Publications](publications.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}  
[Projects and Funding](projects.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}

:::
:::

:::

:::

---

## Mathematical and Computational Modelling Across Quantum, Networked, and Biological Systems

I study systems in which local interactions, contextual constraints, evolving structures, and multi-level organisation shape global behaviour — from quantum and nanoscale processes to communication networks and biological organisation. My work develops geometric, topological, algebraic, and computational frameworks for local-to-global structure, contextuality, evolving networks, models of computation, and biological organisation.

My research connects quantum foundations, quantum information and computation, topological, geometric, and algebraic methods, evolving communication systems, mathematical/theoretical biology, and topology-driven models of computation.

---

## Research at a Glance

The research areas below are organised into three layers: foundational physics, mathematical and computational structures, and complex systems and applications.

---

## Foundational Physics

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### Quantum Foundations

Non-locality, contextuality, Bell–Kochen–Specker models, and philosophical questions in quantum theory, studied through geometric, topological, algebraic, sheaf-theoretic, and bundle/gauge-theoretic methods.

[Explore this area →](quantum-foundations.qmd)

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### Quantum Information and Computation

Contextuality as a structural resource for quantum computation, gauge structure in measurement-based quantum computation, topology-sensitive computation, and mathematical foundations of quantum algorithms and protocols.

[Explore this area →](quantum-information-computation.qmd)

:::
:::
:::

:::

---

## Mathematical and Computational Structures

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### Topological, Geometric, and Algebraic Methods

Geometric and categorical methods for local-to-global structure, including sheaf theory, bundle geometry, simplicial topology, obstruction theory, gauge/holonomy ideas, and related algebraic methods.

[Explore this area →](topological-geometric-algebraic-methods.qmd)

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### Models of Computation

Topology-driven models of computation in which structure and function co-evolve. This direction studies computation as structured interaction, not only as fixed input-output transformation.

[Explore this area →](models-of-computation.qmd)

:::
:::
:::

:::

---

## Complex Systems and Applications

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### Evolving Networks and Communication Systems

Evolving, heterogeneous, and reconfigurable communication systems where topology, configuration state, physical constraints, policies, and operational context change together.

[Explore this area →](evolving-networks-communication-systems.qmd)

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### Mathematical and Theoretical Biology

Constraint-semantic models for multi-level biological organisation, cross-scale biological communication, active interfaces, circular causality, and biological systems beyond fixed state spaces.

[Explore this area →](mathematical-theoretical-biology.qmd)

:::
:::
:::

:::

---

## Explore More

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### [Research](research.qmd)

A structured overview of my research programme and its six main areas.

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### [Publications](publications.qmd)

Peer-reviewed publications, theses, preprints, and public manuscripts.

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### [Projects and Funding](projects.qmd)

Research environments, funding context, and ongoing projects.

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### [CV](cv.qmd)

Academic background, appointments, talks, teaching, supervision, and research activities.

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### [Contact](contact.qmd)

Affiliation, email, and academic links.

:::
:::
:::

:::
